artifacts.blurb:
05/04/9999
TAGS#TUTORIAL#INTRO#ARTIFACT
WARNING: I am working on optimizing this site's performance. I apologize if it makes your PC hot.

1. Hover your mouse over ANYTHING but this tutorial, then scroll "up" to travel back in time.
2. Once an artifact comes "into focus," it will be clickable.
3. Click a artifact to see an info card.
4. Minimize the card by clicking it or another "in focus" artifact.
artifacts.bodyText:
WARNING: I am working on optimizing this site's performance. I apologize if it makes your PC hot.

1. Hover your mouse over ANYTHING but this tutorial, then scroll "up" to travel back in time.
2. Once an artifact comes "into focus," it will be clickable.
3. Click a artifact to see an info card.
4. Minimize the card by clicking it or another "in focus" artifact.

artifacts.blurb:
05/04/9998
TAGS#TUTORIAL#INTRO
This is my ePortfolio where I will store my Human Things.
Here, you will find pictures of projects, food, and other things that I enjoy.

This site uses your GPU to render a power diagram.
Specifically, this site renders the intersection of a 3D weighted Voronoi Diagram with a flat plane.
By scrolling back and forth, you control the height of this plane.
artifacts.bodyText:
This is my ePortfolio where I will store my Human Things.
Here, you will find pictures of projects, food, and other things that I enjoy.

This site uses your GPU to render a power diagram.
Specifically, this site renders the intersection of a 3D weighted Voronoi Diagram with a flat plane.
By scrolling back and forth, you control the height of this plane.

artifacts.blurb:
5/16/2025
TAGS#ROBOTICS#DIY#PERSONAL STATEMENT#RESUME
Presentation of my ROV at the Design, Cultures, and Creativity (DCC) Capstone Fair.
The Boat sits aloft my contraband storage box.
The ROV sits below atop a piece of contraband (3D-Printing Filament).
The ROV's camera feed is displayed on the monitor to my side.
artifacts.bodyText:
Presentation of my ROV at the Design, Cultures, and Creativity (DCC) Capstone Fair.
The Boat sits aloft my contraband storage box.
The ROV sits below atop a piece of contraband (3D-Printing Filament).
The ROV's camera feed is displayed on the monitor to my side.

artifacts.blurb:
05/11/2025
Both Halves of the ROV Electronics, almost completed.
On the left: a Luckfox Pico Plus with ethernet leading outside. Two wires from the ethernet are fed into a 5V regulator.
On the right: the almost-complete power train of the ROV. 5 ESC's, 5 BLDC motors, two control-mosfets, and a PWM light controller.
Both sides feature an intensely powerful epoxy-encased LED.
The two halves interface with a sketchy homebrew plug.
artifacts.bodyText:
Both Halves of the ROV Electronics, almost completed.
On the left: a Luckfox Pico Plus with ethernet leading outside. Two wires from the ethernet are fed into a 5V regulator.
On the right: the almost-complete power train of the ROV. 5 ESC's, 5 BLDC motors, two control-mosfets, and a PWM light controller.
Both sides feature an intensely powerful epoxy-encased LED.
The two halves interface with a sketchy homebrew plug.

artifacts.blurb:
05/08/2025
An empty Boat on my floor, set to become the carrier vessel of my ROV.
Design: Catamaran Airboat with a spool for carrying the ROV's tether.
One motor is capable of driving the spool and the propeller via last-minute gearbox design.
The gearbox uses two sliding clutches to output forward or reverse to one of two outputs.
artifacts.bodyText:
An empty Boat on my floor, set to become the carrier vessel of my ROV.
Design: Catamaran Airboat with a spool for carrying the ROV's tether.
One motor is capable of driving the spool and the propeller via last-minute gearbox design.
The gearbox uses two sliding clutches to output forward or reverse to one of two outputs.

artifacts.blurb:
05/08/2025 - Bathroom Soldering
My ROV's powertrain, halfway through the soldering process.
5 Brushless drone ESC's are wrapped around a single 18650 battery cell.
The 3 output channels of each ESC are routed through an epoxy endcap to breach the ROV's air-water seal.
Enameled copper wire was the only suitable option at this scale, with limited resources.
A single scratch through the water-exposed enamel could compromise the entire unit for long-term use.
I should have just put the ESC's outside the vehicle for cooling, modularity, space, and shorter wires.
artifacts.bodyText:
My ROV's powertrain, halfway through the soldering process.
5 Brushless drone ESC's are wrapped around a single 18650 battery cell.
The 3 output channels of each ESC are routed through an epoxy endcap to breach the ROV's air-water seal.
Enameled copper wire was the only suitable option at this scale, with limited resources.
A single scratch through the water-exposed enamel could compromise the entire unit for long-term use.
I should have just put the ESC's outside the vehicle for cooling, modularity, space, and shorter wires.
